step_by_step_thinking:
Angiofibroma extending to one or more paranasal sinuses would be classified according to the TNM staging system used for sinonasal tumors. Since angiofibromas can extend beyond just the nasal cavity but do so less frequently compared to some other types of sinonasal tumors like adenocarcinomas, when it does involve adjacent structures including paranasal sinuses, it typically qualifies as Stage II due to local invasion outside the confines of the nasal cavity alone.
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about angiofibromas extending to one or more paranasal sinuses, we classify them as Stage II per the TNM staging criteria for sinonasal tumors. This designation accounts for local invasion past the boundaries of the nasal cavity without distant metastasis.
